posts.text:
what the land sees when you acknowledge it


Pop Crave (@PopCrave)

Katy Perry & Justin Trudeau in new photo.




— https://nitter.net/PopCrave/status/2031785997256499481#m

Wow, this study is devastating for cynicism.

Here's a TL;DR:

In studies 1–3, participants indicated they thought cynics would do better on cognitive tasks.
In studies 4–5, cynics were tested and 1 SD of cynicism was associated with 0.25 and 0.17 SDs lower cognitive ability in studies 4 and 5, respectively.
In study 6, cynics were found to be 

- less educated in 29/30 countries
- less literate in 28/30 countries
- less numerate in 29/30 countries
- less computer-literate in 23/26 countries
Cyn

I wonder what part of Norway they’re from.


Faytuks Network (@FaytuksNetwork)

BREAKING: Norwegian police have arrested three brothers suspected of carrying out the terror bombing outside the U.S. Embassy in Oslo.



— https://nitter.net/FaytuksNetwork/status/2031770776311894442#m
This meme is from 2011 btw

"All things are common among us but our wives. We give up our community where it is practised alone by others, who not only take possession of the wives of their friends, but most tolerantly also accommodate their friends with theirs...." —Tertullian on Christians vs. pagans


New York Post (@nypost)

Could 'hotwifing' save your marriage? 71% of kinky couples say loaning their wives out for sex 'strengthened' their union trib.al/Zy4es0x

the richard nixon foundation scares me because you could whisper his name and the intern would quotetweet and mog you with an edit or something 😭



— https://nitter.net/frankidacre/status/2031555982644678826#m

I give it 6 months before the Michael Knowles show is shilling the wing flavored protein espresso martini candie. I’m there for it.

It's about time.

Peerage by inheritance is finally gone, replaced with peerage by purchase and influence. And the ghastly meritocratic class calls this progress.

👍👍👍🇺🇸🇺🇸🇺🇸


David Sivak (@DISivak)

News: Thune tells me he plans to bring the SAVE America Act to the floor next week



— https://nitter.net/DISivak/status/2031439611411444140#m

It was with real sadness that I learned that the monks of the Abbey of La Trappe in Normandy are considering closing down their operation. As the name suggests, this monastery is the mother house of the Trappist Order, a reform of the Cistercian movement and a particularly intense form of Benedictine life, famous for its austerity and silence. I came to know of La Trappe through Thomas Merton, a Trappist monk of the Abbey of Gethsemani in Kentucky, whom I read with great passion when I was a you
